content.problemStatement:
Steps to insert a word in trie: 
- We start at the root and set a variable current to the root.
- we iterate over the word and for each character ch in the word we:
    - get the index that represents that character
    - if that index of the children array does not contain a pointer to the next node we create a new trie node to save the pointer at that index.
    - we update the current variable to the next node.
- we set the end value of the current node to true.

Let's follow these steps to insert the word "cats" in the following trie:
```
      [ c     d ]
       /       \
     [a]       [o]
     /           \
  [ l  t ]       [g]
   /    \          \
 [l]    [end]   [end]
 /        
[end]     

```
- we set current = [c, d]
- we iterate on the word "cats"
    - ch = c , the index of c contains a pointer
    - we set current to the pointer at index of c
    - ch = a , the index of a contains a pointer
    - we set current to the pointer at index of a
    - ch = t , the index of t contains a pointer
    - we set current to the pointer at index of t
    - ch = s, the index of s does not contain a pointer
    - we create a new node and set the pointer denoting s to that new node
    - we set current to the new node
- we set the end of current node to true.

```
      [ c     d ]
       /       \
     [a]       [o]
     /           \
  [ l  t ]       [g]
   /    \          \
 [l]    [[s],end]  [end]
 /        \
[end]     [end]

```

Inspect the insert function in the code editor to see how to insert a word in a trie structure.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ — the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
